task: Implement Day 3 (D3-01..D3-09) from docs/task-breakdown.md — Spring Security + OAuth2 social login (Facebook/Twitter) with CSRF, custom OAuth2UserService upserting User+SocialAccount, login page, post-login redirect, integration tests
acceptanceCriteria: spring-boot-starter-security + oauth2-client dependencies added; project compiles on Spring Boot 4.1.0 / Java 26 (D3-01)
SecurityConfig enables CSRF, permits /login, /oauth2/**, /login/oauth2/**, /swagger-ui/**, /v3/api-docs/**; oauth2Login with success/failure handlers (D3-02)
CSRF token available to templates/REST clients (meta tag or cookie repo) (D3-03)
application.properties has Facebook + Twitter client registrations (env-var backed, boots with placeholders) (D3-04)
Custom OAuth2UserService extracts provider user info and upserts User + SocialAccount storing access_token (D3-05)
GET /login renders page with Facebook and Twitter login buttons (D3-06)
Forms carry CSRF token; tampered/missing CSRF token requests rejected 403 (D3-08)
Integration tests: unauthenticated protected endpoint -> 302 to login; authenticated (oauth2Login post-processor) -> 200 (D3-09)
Upserted users get role explicitly set (never rely on ADMIN default) — deferred-trap #4 from 260710 review
Access tokens never logged; hibernate bind logging stays <= DEBUG
Full existing test suite still passes (existing controller slice tests must cope with security filters)
touchpoints: pom.xml (add security, oauth2-client, thymeleaf, thymeleaf-extras-springsecurity, spring-security-test deps)
src/test/java/ (security integration tests, new; existing @WebMvcTest slices may need security config import)
blastRadius: ALL existing endpoints (/posts, /metrics, /import-posts, /export-report, swagger) become authenticated — existing @WebMvcTest slice tests will start failing 401/403 unless configured
POST/DELETE endpoints (DELETE /posts/{id}, POST /import-posts) now require CSRF token — API consumers affected
Springdoc swagger-ui must remain reachable per D3-02 permitted paths
No dashboard page exists yet (D4/D5 scope) — post-login redirect / currently has no UI; login flow must still redirect there
contracts: docs/database-design.md: users/social_accounts schema (Flyway V1 already has token columns; ddl-auto=none — no schema change expected)
docs/task-breakdown.md D3 tasks are the spec of record

Deferred security traps from plans/reports/*260710*day1-foundation.md: XFF trusted-proxies, bind-log token leak, token encryption at rest (design doc), role DEFAULT ADMIN fail-open, dev-profile default
Twitter/X OAuth2 requires manual provider registration + PKCE (not a Spring CommonOAuth2Provider)
